economic_notes: Nation has economic development activities including Homalco Holdings Limited Partnership, Homalco Wildlife Tours Inc., Port Neville Logging Company, and Homalco Resources Management Limited Partnership. Economic Development segment generated $4,318,569 surplus in 2022.
notes: Audit is qualified for two reasons: (1) no approved budget was prepared for the year as required by PSAS, and (2) financial information for GBEs was unaudited and some related party information was unavailable. Four entities fully consolidated (Bute Inlet Development Corporation, Homalco Holdings Inc., Homalco Trust, 1339092 BC Ltd., 1340838 BC Ltd., 1350673 BC Ltd.) and four GBEs accounted for by modified equity method. Nation may be contingently liable for $328,331 in ISC recoveries related to a forensic audit covering April 2012 to November 2017. Total government transfers calculated as ISC $3,551,529 + FNHA $1,187,775 + Province of BC $664,341 + DFO $454,855 + CMHC $84,679 + BC Treaty Commission $375,000 + BC FN Gaming Revenue $211,442 + First Peoples Cultural Council $290,852 = $6,820,473 less Ottawa Trust $1,073 included separately; OSR adjusted accordingly. Current liabilities include bank indebtedness $11,300 + accounts payable $981,392 = $992,692 excluding deferred revenue and long-term debt.
